articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• Government launches the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) with enhanced focus on export markets and advanced R&D.
• New incentives introduced for establishing Green Hydrogen hubs and promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolysers.
• Aims to achieve a target of 10 MMT (million metric tonnes) green hydrogen production capacity by 2035, exceeding initial goals.
• Emphasizes job creation, skill development, and fostering a robust ecosystem for green hydrogen technologies.
• Positions India as a key player in the global energy transition and a major exporter of green hydrogen and its derivatives.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Conservation
Science & Technology-developments & their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) was initially launched in 2023.
Initial target: 5 MMT green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
Phase II targets 10 MMT production capacity by 2035.
SIGHT program (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) is a key component offering financial incentives.
Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity (electrolysis).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy for energy security and decarbonization through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential, including job creation, export revenue, and industrial transformation.
Examine challenges: cost competitiveness, infrastructure development (storage, transport), and technological advancements.
Evaluate the role of government incentives, R&D, and public-private partnerships in achieving NGHM goals.
Impact on India's geopolitical standing and contribution to global climate action.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image showing a green hydrogen production plant with solar panels and wind turbines in the background, symbolizing renewable energy integration.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Amendments Ensuring Greater Transparency in Electoral Bonds
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld recent legislative amendments aimed at enhancing transparency in the electoral bond scheme.
• The ruling mandates disclosure of donor identities to the Election Commission of India (ECI) for all future donations.
• This decision comes after prolonged public debate and multiple petitions challenging the opacity of the original scheme.
• Advocates for electoral reforms hail the verdict as a significant step towards cleaning political funding.
• The court emphasized the citizen's right to know and the importance of a level playing field in democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bond Scheme was introduced in 2017.
Initially, donor identity was kept anonymous.
Supreme Court had previously struck down certain aspects of the original scheme.
The current ruling upholds amendments requiring disclosure of donor details to ECI.
Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression, which includes the right to know.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on electoral funding transparency in India.
Analyze the balance between donor privacy and the public's right to information in political donations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of electoral bonds as a mechanism for political funding and its impact on democratic principles.
Suggest further reforms needed to ensure fair and transparent elections in India.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional values related to democratic governance.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with the Supreme Court of India building in the background, symbolizing judicial pronouncement.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for administering elections in India according to the rules and regulations mentioned in the Constitution of India.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Renewed Focus on Maritime Security and Digital Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit wrapped up, solidifying strategic partnerships in key areas.
• Leaders emphasized enhanced cooperation in maritime domain awareness, counter-piracy, and freedom of navigation.
• Significant agreements signed to boost digital economy integration, cybersecurity, and cross-border digital payments.
• India reiterated its commitment to the Act East Policy, seeking deeper economic and cultural ties with Southeast Asian nations.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, climate change, and post-pandemic economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was founded in 1967.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Full Dialogue Partner in 1995.
India's 'Look East Policy' was upgraded to 'Act East Policy' in 2014.
ASEAN members include Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam.
Maritime security and digital cooperation are increasingly important pillars of India-ASEAN relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of ASEAN for India's Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN nations.
Examine how digital cooperation can drive economic growth and regional integration in Southeast Asia.
Evaluate India's role in addressing common regional challenges such as climate change and geopolitical shifts.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the India-ASEAN comprehensive strategic partnership.
articles.imageDescription: A composite image showing flags of India and ASEAN member states, with naval ships and digital network lines symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The ASEAN Secretariat is located in Jakarta, Indonesia.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Stable Inflation, Signals Focus on Sustained Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged in its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting.
• The move comes as retail inflation remains within the target range, providing flexibility for growth-oriented policies.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic uncertainties and the need for domestic growth impulses to be nurtured.
• Analysts anticipate continued liquidity management measures to support credit flow in the economy.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to price stability while supporting the government's development agenda.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
RBI's inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The MPC consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Analyze the interplay between inflation control and economic growth in India's monetary policy framework.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy tools in addressing India's economic challenges.
Examine the impact of global economic factors on RBI's domestic policy decisions.
Suggest how fiscal and monetary policies can be better coordinated to achieve sustainable development goals.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai, with a graph showing stable economic indicators in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Union Government Approves Comprehensive Coastal Zone Management Plan for Vulnerable Regions
articles.explainer:
• A new, updated Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) has been approved, focusing on climate resilience and sustainable development.
• The plan incorporates advanced geospatial mapping and climate vulnerability assessments for sensitive coastal areas.
• It introduces stricter regulations for construction and industrial activities in ecologically fragile zones.
• Emphasizes livelihood protection for fishing communities and promotes eco-tourism initiatives.
• Aims to balance economic development with environmental conservation in India's extensive coastline.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) notifications are issued under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
CRZ rules regulate development activities along India's coastline.
CZMPs are prepared by coastal states/UTs based on CRZ notifications.
The plan categorizes coastal areas into CRZ-I (ecologically sensitive), CRZ-II (developed areas), CRZ-III (undeveloped areas), and CRZ-IV (water area).
Mangroves, coral reefs, and sand dunes are critical coastal ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of an updated Coastal Zone Management Plan in the context of climate change and rising sea levels.
Analyze the challenges in implementing CZMPs, considering stakeholder conflicts and diverse coastal ecosystems.
Evaluate the balance between protecting coastal ecology and promoting economic activities like tourism and fisheries.
Suggest innovative approaches for community participation and technology integration in coastal management.
Examine the plan's potential impact on the livelihoods of coastal communities and marine biodiversity.
articles.imageDescription: A serene coastal landscape featuring mangroves, a sandy beach, and fishing boats, illustrating the diverse elements of a coastal zone.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of approximately 7,516 km, including the mainland and island territories.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a major milestone with the successful autonomous landing of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• This critical test validates key technologies for India's future space missions, aiming to reduce launch costs significantly.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) performed a precise landing on a simulated runway after being released from an aerial platform.
• Paves the way for India to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• Further enhances India's capabilities in advanced space technology and commercial space services.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop reusable launch vehicle technology.
The test involved an autonomous landing of the RLV demonstrator.
Reusable launch vehicles significantly reduce the cost of space access.
SpaceX's Falcon 9 is a prominent example of a reusable launch vehicle.
India's Gaganyaan mission is a human spaceflight program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how RLV development contributes to reducing launch costs and enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects for ISRO in developing a fully reusable space transportation system.
Examine the broader implications of such technological advancements for national security and scientific research.
Suggest policy measures to further boost private sector participation in India's space industry.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustration of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD) making an autonomous landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: ISRO was formed in 1969, succeeding the Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR).

articles.headline: Government Rolls Out Universal Healthcare Access Program Pilot in 10 States
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry launched a pilot phase of its ambitious 'Sarvajan Swasthya Yojana' (Universal Healthcare Scheme) in ten states.
• The program aims to provide comprehensive health coverage, including preventive, promotive, curative, and palliative care, to all citizens.
• It integrates existing national health schemes and focuses on strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure.
• A digital health ID system is being implemented to ensure seamless access to medical records and services.
• The pilot phase will assess implementation challenges and gather feedback before a nationwide rollout.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is a flagship health scheme providing health insurance cover.
National Health Mission (NHM) encompasses both rural and urban health missions.
Digital Health ID is part of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
The pilot program focuses on comprehensive healthcare, including preventive and promotive aspects.
Right to health is often linked to Article 21 (Right to Life) of the Indian Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need and challenges of achieving universal healthcare access in a diverse country like India.
Analyze how the 'Sarvajan Swasthya Yojana' integrates with and improves upon existing health schemes.
Evaluate the role of digital health infrastructure and data privacy concerns in its implementation.
Examine the potential impact of such a scheme on public health indicators, poverty reduction, and human development.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and quality healthcare delivery across all regions and socio-economic strata.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of people from different age groups and backgrounds receiving medical consultation, symbolizing universal healthcare.
articles.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 aims to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a comprehensive healthcare system.

articles.headline: Comprehensive Cybersecurity Policy 2025 Unveiled to Combat Growing Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cybersecurity Policy 2025.
• The policy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and critical national infrastructure.
• It emphasizes public-private partnerships, skill development, and international cooperation to tackle advanced cyber threats.
• Establishes a framework for incident response, data protection, and robust regulatory mechanisms.
• Focuses on promoting indigenous cybersecurity solutions and strengthening the 'Cyber Surakshit Bharat' initiative.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: India's first National Cyber Security Policy was released in 2013.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to cybersecurity incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets essential for national security, economy, and public health.
The policy emphasizes 'Cyber Surakshit Bharat' initiative for capacity building.
Data Protection Bill (now Act) is crucial for safeguarding personal data in cyberspace.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and the need for a robust National Cybersecurity Policy in India.
Analyze the key pillars of the National Cybersecurity Policy 2025 and its potential impact on national security and economic stability.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing the policy, including technological gaps, human resource shortages, and inter-agency coordination.
Examine the role of international cooperation and data governance frameworks in strengthening India's cyber resilience.
Suggest measures to foster a culture of cybersecurity awareness among citizens and organizations.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network, with a shield icon overlayed on a map of India, symbolizing national cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyberattacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to internal security through communication networks

articles.headline: India Advocates for Urgent UN Security Council Reforms at G4 Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister strongly reiterated the call for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC) at the G4 meeting.
• The G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, India, and Japan) emphasized the need for an expanded UNSC to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• Highlighted the underrepresentation of developing countries, particularly from Africa, Latin America, and Asia, in the permanent membership.
• India stressed its credentials for a permanent seat, citing its demographic size, economic power, and commitment to multilateralism.
• The meeting concluded with a joint statement urging accelerated intergovernmental negotiations on UNSC expansion.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: The UN Security Council has 15 members: 5 permanent (P5) and 10 non-permanent.
The P5 are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
Non-permanent members are elected for two-year terms by the UN General Assembly.
G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, India, Japan) advocate for permanent seats for themselves.
India has served multiple terms as a non-permanent member of the UNSC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of the UN Security Council's permanent membership.
Analyze India's aspirations for a permanent UNSC seat and the challenges it faces in achieving this goal.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the G4 group in pushing for UNSC reforms and its role in global governance.
Examine how the current structure of the UNSC reflects or fails to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
Suggest pathways and strategies for India to strengthen its claim for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York, with flags of member states, symbolizing international diplomacy and reform efforts.
articles.trivia: The UN Charter was signed on 26 June 1945, in San Francisco, at the conclusion of the United Nations Conference on International Organization.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for ACC Battery Manufacturing Attracts Significant New Investments
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery storage has successfully attracted several new domestic and international investors.
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries announced the approval of fresh proposals, boosting India's capacity in critical battery technologies.
• This initiative is crucial for accelerating India's electric vehicle (EV) adoption and renewable energy storage goals.
• The investments are expected to create thousands of jobs and reduce reliance on battery imports.
• Reinforces India's commitment to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in the high-growth EV and energy storage sectors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing was approved in 2021.
It aims to achieve 50 GWh (Gigawatt Hour) of ACC manufacturing capacity.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
The scheme provides incentives based on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
India is heavily dependent on imports for critical battery components and cells.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing in achieving India's EV and renewable energy targets.
Analyze the economic benefits, including job creation, import substitution, and fostering a domestic manufacturing ecosystem.
Evaluate the challenges faced by India in scaling up ACC battery production, such as raw material access and technological expertise.
Examine the role of government policies in driving industrial growth and technological self-reliance.
Suggest further policy interventions to make India a global hub for battery manufacturing and innovation.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of a battery cell manufacturing plant with robots and advanced machinery, symbolizing high-tech production.
articles.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries were first commercialized by Sony in 1991.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Completes 52 Years: New Conservation Strategies Discussed Amidst Climate Challenges
articles.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, marked its 52nd anniversary, celebrating significant achievements in tiger population recovery.
• A national conference reviewed existing strategies and discussed new approaches to tackle emerging threats like climate change and habitat fragmentation.
• Emphasis placed on human-wildlife conflict mitigation, corridor protection, and community involvement in conservation efforts.
• New technologies, including AI-powered surveillance and genetic monitoring, are proposed for enhanced protection.
• India reaffirms its commitment to the 'Tx2' goal of doubling wild tiger populations.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
It is administered by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA).
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
The 'Tx2' goal aims to double the wild tiger population by 2022 (initially set in 2010), which India largely achieved.
Tiger reserves are managed based on a core-buffer strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the successes and challenges faced by Project Tiger over its 52-year journey in conserving big cats.
Analyze the impact of climate change, habitat loss, and human-wildlife conflict on tiger populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of community-based conservation and technological interventions in wildlife protection.
Examine the economic and ecological benefits of tiger conservation for local communities and ecosystems.
Suggest future strategies for sustainable tiger conservation, considering landscape-level approaches and international cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger walking through dense forest, symbolizing India's conservation success.
articles.trivia: India has 54 tiger reserves spread across 18 states, covering approximately 2.3% of the country's geographical area.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Completes Draft Report for a North-Eastern State, Sparks Debate
articles.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission submitted its draft report for redrawing assembly and parliamentary constituencies in a key North-Eastern state.
• The exercise aims to rationalize constituency boundaries based on the latest census data and geographical contiguity.
• The report has generated significant political and social debate, with various groups raising concerns over representation and demographic shifts.
• Public hearings are scheduled to gather feedback from stakeholders before the final report is published.
• The delimitation process is crucial for ensuring fair representation in legislative bodies and upholding democratic principles.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Delimitation means the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission of India.
Its main function is to redraw the boundaries of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituencies.
The last nationwide delimitation exercise was completed in 2002-2008.
Delimitation is done on the basis of the latest census data, but the number of seats for Lok Sabha and Assemblies was frozen until 2026 by the 84th Amendment Act, 2001.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and objectives behind the delimitation exercise in India.
Analyze the socio-political implications of delimitation, particularly in ethnically diverse regions like the North-East.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in ensuring fair and equitable representation.
Examine the potential for conflicts and discontent arising from the redrawing of constituency boundaries.
Suggest measures to make the delimitation process more transparent and acceptable to all stakeholders.
articles.imageDescription: A map of a North-Eastern state with constituency boundaries being redrawn, symbolizing the delimitation process.
articles.trivia: The orders of the Delimitation Commission have the force of law and cannot be called in question in any court.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Skill Development Progress
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation found significant progress in integrating skill development.
• The Ministry of Education highlighted increased enrollment in vocational courses and multidisciplinary programs at various levels.
• Focus on experiential learning, critical thinking, and digital literacy has shown positive early outcomes.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access across all regions.
• The review emphasized continued efforts to align education with industry demands and prepare students for future jobs.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key features include 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, multidisciplinary approach, vocational integration, and flexible learning.
Focuses on foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
Promotes the use of technology in education, including National Educational Technology Forum (NETF).
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 for India's education system.
Analyze the progress made in integrating skill development and vocational education as envisioned by NEP.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of NEP 2020, especially concerning teacher training, infrastructure, and digital divide.
Examine how NEP aims to align education with the demands of the 21st-century job market and foster innovation.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access and quality education for all, particularly in rural and remote areas.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on learning activities in a modern classroom, symbolizing skill-based education.
articles.trivia: India's first National Policy on Education was formulated in 1968, based on the recommendations of the Kothari Commission.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of 5G-Advanced Technology, India Set for Global Role
articles.explainer:
• Indian researchers and telecom firms announced a major breakthrough in developing indigenous 5G-Advanced (5G-A) technology.
• The advancements include enhanced capabilities for IoT, AI integration, and improved energy efficiency for telecom networks.
• This achievement positions India as a key contributor to global 6G standards development.
• The Ministry of Communications highlighted the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in telecommunications and digital infrastructure.
• Expected to catalyze new applications in smart cities, autonomous vehicles, and remote healthcare.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: 5G-Advanced is the next stage of 5G evolution, leading towards 6G.
It focuses on enhanced mobile broadband, ultra-reliable low-latency communications, and massive machine-type communications.
India launched its 5G services in 2022.
The 'Bharat 6G Alliance' was launched to foster R&D in 6G technology.
Indigenous development reduces reliance on foreign technology and enhances national security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous 5G-Advanced technology development for India's digital transformation and economic growth.
Analyze how this breakthrough positions India in the global race for next-generation communication technologies (e.g., 6G).
Evaluate the potential applications and societal impact of 5G-A in sectors like smart cities, healthcare, and industry.
Examine the challenges in deploying advanced telecom infrastructure across India, including spectrum management and rural connectivity.
Suggest policy measures to encourage further innovation and private sector investment in the telecom R&D ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic network graphic with glowing lines connecting cities and devices, representing advanced 5G technology.
articles.trivia: The first generation of cellular technology (1G) was introduced in the 1980s.

articles.headline: India and EU Sign Critical Minerals Partnership Agreement, Boosting Supply Chain Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India and the European Union (EU) formalized a strategic partnership agreement on critical minerals, aiming to diversify supply chains.
• The pact focuses on joint exploration, sustainable extraction, processing, and recycling of crucial raw materials.
• Recognizes the importance of critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earths for green and digital transitions.
• Aims to reduce dependency on single-source suppliers and enhance economic security for both regions.
• Establishes a framework for technology transfer, research collaboration, and investment in the critical minerals sector.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Critical minerals are essential for modern technologies and economic growth, particularly in green and digital sectors.
Examples include lithium (EV batteries), cobalt (EV batteries), and rare earth elements (electronics).
India and the EU are both heavily reliant on imports for many critical minerals.
This partnership aims to reduce geopolitical risks associated with concentrated supply chains.
The EU has its own Critical Raw Materials Act to secure supply.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth and national security in the context of global supply chain vulnerabilities.
Analyze how the India-EU partnership on critical minerals can enhance supply chain resilience and foster a sustainable green transition for both regions.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in joint exploration, extraction, processing, and recycling of critical minerals.
Examine the geopolitical implications of such partnerships in reducing dependency on dominant suppliers.
Suggest policy frameworks for India to build domestic capabilities in critical mineral value chains and promote circular economy principles.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized geological cross-section showing layers of earth with various critical minerals highlighted, alongside the flags of India and the EU.
articles.trivia: China currently dominates the global supply chain for many critical minerals, particularly rare earth elements.

articles.headline: Urban Heat Island Effect: Major Indian Cities Adopt Mitigation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• Several major Indian cities have begun implementing comprehensive strategies to combat the escalating Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect.
• Measures include increasing green cover, promoting cool roofs and pavements, and improving urban planning for better ventilation.
• The initiatives are part of broader climate resilience plans, aiming to reduce heat-related health risks and energy consumption.
• Collaboration between municipal corporations, urban development bodies, and environmental experts is key to success.
• Public awareness campaigns are also being launched to encourage citizen participation in UHI mitigation efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.
Causes include dark surfaces (asphalt, concrete) absorbing solar radiation, lack of vegetation, and waste heat from human activities.
Mitigation strategies include increasing tree cover, using reflective materials (cool roofs), and permeable pavements.
UHI contributes to increased energy demand for cooling and adverse health impacts.
Smart Cities Mission includes components for green infrastructure and sustainable urban development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Analyze the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies, such as green infrastructure and cool surfaces, in combating UHI.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing UHI mitigation plans, including land availability, funding, and public participation.
Examine the broader implications of UHI for public health, energy consumption, and climate resilience in urban areas.
Suggest integrated urban planning approaches that combine UHI mitigation with sustainable development goals for Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A split image showing a dense urban area with high temperatures on one side and a greener, cooler urban area with trees and reflective roofs on the other.
articles.trivia: A study found that the UHI effect can increase urban temperatures by several degrees Celsius compared to surrounding rural areas.

articles.headline: Agricultural Export Growth Targets Revised Upwards for 2025-26 Amidst Global Demand Surge
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced an upward revision of India's agricultural export growth targets for the fiscal year 2025-26.
• The revised targets reflect growing global demand for Indian agricultural products and improved domestic supply chain efficiencies.
• Focus areas include high-value products, organic produce, and processed foods, with an emphasis on quality and certification.
• Government initiatives like Agri-Export Zones and the 'One District One Product' scheme are driving this growth.
• Aims to enhance farmers' income, strengthen India's position in global food trade, and diversify export baskets.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, – different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India is a major global producer of several agricultural commodities.
The Agricultural Export Policy 2018 aims to double agricultural exports by 2022 (initial target).
Agri-Export Zones (AEZs) were established to boost agricultural exports from specific geographical areas.
The 'One District One Product' (ODOP) initiative promotes specialized products from each district.
Processed foods and organic products have high export potential.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the upward revision of India's agricultural export targets and its implications for the economy.
Analyze how government policies and schemes are facilitating growth in agricultural exports and improving farmers' income.
Evaluate the challenges in scaling up agricultural exports, including infrastructure gaps, quality standards, and market access.
Examine the role of food processing industries and value addition in boosting export potential.
Suggest strategies for India to diversify its agricultural export basket and enhance its competitiveness in global markets.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant image of various fresh Indian agricultural produce, such as spices, fruits, and vegetables, arranged for export.
articles.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of milk, pulses, and spices.

articles.headline: Lokpal Receives Record Number of Corruption Complaints; Efficiency Praised
articles.explainer:
• The Lokpal of India reported a record number of corruption complaints received in the current fiscal year, indicating increased public trust.
• The institution's efficiency in processing and investigating cases was highlighted in its annual report.
• Focus on proactive measures, including digital platforms for complaint filing and transparent procedures.
• The Lokpal emphasized its role in promoting good governance and accountability in public administration.
• Continued efforts are underway to strengthen the institution's capacity and outreach across the country.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of a Lokpal for the Union and Lokayukta for States.
The first Lokpal of India, Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose, was appointed in 2019.
Lokpal investigates allegations of corruption against public functionaries, including the Prime Minister (with safeguards), ministers, and MPs.
The institution aims to provide a mechanism for citizens to complain against corruption.
The concept of an ombudsman originated in Sweden.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Lokpal institution in combating corruption and promoting good governance in India.
Analyze the factors contributing to the increased number of complaints received by the Lokpal and its implications for public trust.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Lokpal in its functioning, such as jurisdictional limitations and implementation delays.
Examine the relationship between the Lokpal and other anti-corruption bodies in India's governance framework.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the independence, effectiveness, and outreach of the Lokpal institution.
articles.imageDescription: A symbolic image of a balance scale with a gavel, representing justice and accountability, with the Lokpal building in the background.
articles.trivia: The idea of an ombudsman was first proposed in India by the then Law Minister Ashok Kumar Sen in Parliament in the early 1960s.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Surpasses Pre-Pandemic Levels, Boosted by Domestic Travel and E-Visa Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector has fully recovered and surpassed pre-pandemic visitor numbers, driven primarily by robust domestic travel.
• Government's 'Dekho Apna Desh' initiative and streamlined e-visa processes for international tourists have played a crucial role.
• Focus on niche tourism segments like adventure, spiritual, and medical tourism is attracting diverse visitors.
• Significant investment in infrastructure development, including regional airports and road networks, supports accessibility.
• The resurgence is contributing significantly to job creation and economic growth across various states.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: 'Dekho Apna Desh' is an initiative launched by the Ministry of Tourism to encourage citizens to travel within the country.
India offers e-visa facilities to citizens of many countries.
The tourism sector is a significant contributor to India's GDP and employment.
Swadesh Darshan and PRASAD schemes focus on developing tourism infrastructure.
Medical tourism is a growing segment in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors contributing to the remarkable recovery and growth of India's tourism sector post-pandemic.
Analyze the role of government initiatives and policy reforms in boosting both domestic and international tourism.
Evaluate the economic and social benefits of a thriving tourism sector, including job creation and cultural exchange.
Examine the challenges that remain, such as infrastructure gaps, sustainability concerns, and regional disparities in tourism development.
Suggest strategies for India to further enhance its global tourism competitiveness and promote responsible tourism practices.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments, natural landscapes, and cultural festivals, filled with happy tourists.
articles.trivia: The World Tourism Organization (UNWTO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for the promotion of responsible, sustainable and universally accessible tourism.

articles.headline: India's First Fully Private Sector-Developed Earth Observation Satellite Successfully Launched
articles.explainer:
• A historic moment for India's space sector as the first fully private-sector-developed Earth Observation (EO) satellite was successfully launched.
• Developed by an Indian startup with support from ISRO, the satellite will provide high-resolution imagery for various applications.
• This launch marks a significant step towards greater private participation and innovation in India's space economy.
• The data from the satellite is expected to benefit agriculture, urban planning, disaster management, and environmental monitoring.
• Underscores the success of recent space sector reforms aimed at fostering a vibrant commercial space ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre) was created to promote and regulate private sector participation in the space sector.
Earth Observation satellites provide data for various applications like weather forecasting, agriculture, and disaster management.
India's space sector reforms aim to open up opportunities for private companies in space activities.
ISRO has been launching satellites for other countries for commercial purposes.
Small satellite technology is a growing trend in the private space sector.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of increased private sector participation in India's space program and its potential for economic growth.
Analyze how the launch of India's first private EO satellite contributes to the 'NewSpace' ecosystem and technological innovation.
Evaluate the benefits of high-resolution Earth observation data for various sectors, including agriculture, environment, and urban planning.
Examine the role of ISRO and government policies in facilitating and regulating private space ventures.
Suggest measures to further boost investment, R&D, and skill development in India's emerging private space industry.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space, with a small satellite orbiting Earth in the foreground, symbolizing private sector contribution to space exploration.
articles.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched in 1975 by the Soviet Union.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Space Sector Reforms and Private Participation

articles.headline: New Species of Freshwater Fish Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian ichthyologists announced the discovery of a new species of freshwater fish in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The discovery underscores the immense, yet underexplored, biodiversity of this UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the unique ecosystems of the region.
• The Western Ghats are known for their high endemism and serve as a crucial watershed for peninsular India.
• The finding contributes to the growing scientific understanding of aquatic biodiversity in the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Known for high levels of endemism (species found nowhere else).
It is older than the Himalayas and influences India's monsoon weather pattern.
Threats include habitat fragmentation, deforestation, and climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and its role in India's ecosystem services.
Analyze the threats to biodiversity in the Western Ghats, including human activities and climate change.
Evaluate the importance of such discoveries for scientific research and conservation planning.
Examine the challenges in protecting endemic species and fragile ecosystems in a rapidly developing region.
Suggest measures for sustainable management and community-based conservation initiatives in the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of the newly discovered freshwater fish species swimming in clear water, with lush green Western Ghats scenery in the background.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: India's First Underwater Metro Expands Operations, Enhancing Urban Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The country's first underwater metro line, located in Kolkata, announced the expansion of its operational services to new routes.
• This engineering marvel significantly enhances urban connectivity and reduces commute times for millions of commuters.
• The project utilizes advanced tunneling technology and strict safety protocols for underwater operations.
• Part of India's broader push for modernizing public transportation infrastructure in metropolitan areas.
• Demonstrates indigenous capabilities in complex infrastructure projects and sustainable urban mobility solutions.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: Kolkata Metro is India's first operational metro railway, started in 1984.
The underwater section of the Kolkata Metro East-West corridor passes under the Hooghly River.
The tunnel is designed to withstand seismic activity and water pressure.
Metro rail projects are crucial for decongesting urban areas and reducing pollution.
Public-Private Partnership (PPP) models are often used for such large-scale infrastructure projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's first underwater metro in transforming urban transportation and connectivity.
Analyze the engineering challenges and technological advancements involved in constructing and expanding an underwater metro line.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of such infrastructure projects on urban development, commuting patterns, and quality of life.
Examine the role of sustainable urban mobility solutions in addressing the challenges of rapid urbanization in Indian cities.
Suggest policy measures to replicate and scale up advanced public transportation projects across other metropolitan areas in India.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek metro train emerging from an underwater tunnel, with the Hooghly River and Kolkata skyline in the background.
articles.trivia: The Hooghly River is a distributary of the Ganges River in West Bengal, India.

articles.headline: National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems Shows Significant Progress
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS) has reported significant advancements in fostering R&D and skill development.
• Focus on areas like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Robotics, and IoT for societal and industrial applications.
• Several Technology Innovation Hubs (TIHs) established under the mission are driving cutting-edge research and startup incubation.
• Aims to create a strong ecosystem for Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) technologies, crucial for Industry 4.0.
• Positions India as a global leader in emerging technologies and contributes to national strategic capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
keyTerms: National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS)
prelimsPoints: NM-ICPS was launched by the Department of Science & Technology (DST) in 2018.
It aims to establish 15 Technology Innovation Hubs (TIHs) across India.
Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) are integrations of computation, networking, and physical processes.
CPS technologies are foundational for Industry 4.0, smart infrastructure, and autonomous systems.
The mission focuses on creating a strong workforce and R&D ecosystem in CPS.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS) for India's technological future.
Analyze how advancements in CPS technologies like AI, Robotics, and IoT can transform various sectors of the Indian economy and society.
Evaluate the role of Technology Innovation Hubs (TIHs) in fostering research, innovation, and startup ecosystem development.
Examine the challenges in developing and deploying advanced CPS technologies, including ethical considerations and data security.
Suggest policy measures to accelerate skill development and interdisciplinary collaboration to make India a global leader in CPS.
articles.imageDescription: A network of interconnected digital brains and robotic arms, symbolizing the integration of AI, IoT, and robotics in Cyber-Physical Systems.
articles.trivia: The concept of Cyber-Physical Systems emerged from the convergence of embedded systems, control theory, and computer science.

articles.headline: Union Government Launches New Scheme for Social Security and Welfare of Migrant Workers
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour & Employment introduced a new comprehensive scheme, 'Shramik Suraksha Abhiyan', for migrant workers.
• The scheme aims to provide portable social security benefits, including health insurance, pension, and housing assistance.
• Focus on creating a national database of migrant workers to ensure seamless access to welfare programs.
• Addresses challenges faced by migrant populations, such as lack of formal employment, exploitation, and limited access to public services.
• Aims to integrate migrant workers into the formal economy and ensure their dignity and rights.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The Inter-State Migrant Workmen (Regulation of Employment and Conditions of Service) Act, 1979, aims to regulate the employment of inter-state migrant workmen.
E-Shram portal was launched to create a national database of unorganized workers.
Social security benefits for unorganized workers are often limited.
The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the vulnerabilities of migrant workers.
The scheme emphasizes portability of benefits across states.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic challenges faced by migrant workers in India and the need for comprehensive social security measures.
Analyze the key features of the 'Shramik Suraksha Abhiyan' and its potential to improve the welfare of migrant populations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of a national database and portable benefits in addressing the unique issues of inter-state migration.
Examine the role of government, employers, and civil society in ensuring the rights and dignity of migrant workers.
Suggest further policy interventions and legal reforms to integrate migrant workers into the formal economy and provide them with equitable opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse workers, some with tools, standing together, symbolizing collective welfare and protection for migrant workers.
articles.trivia: India has one of the largest numbers of internal migrants in the world, with millions moving for work annually.

highlights: India launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting global leadership and enhanced R&D.
Supreme Court upholds amendments ensuring greater transparency in electoral bonds, mandating donor disclosure to ECI.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes with renewed focus on maritime security and digital cooperation, strengthening Act East Policy.
ISRO successfully tests next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing technology, aiming for significant cost reduction in space missions.
Union Government rolls out Universal Healthcare Access Program pilot in 10 states, integrating existing schemes for comprehensive coverage.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM), consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production and export of green hydrogen.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) program provides financial incentives under NGHM.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity, resulting in zero greenhouse gas emissions.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The NGHM aims to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen, the SIGHT program is a key component providing incentives, and the definition of green hydrogen involves electrolysis using renewable energy, hence zero emissions.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Governors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory body of the Reserve Bank of India, responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) Notifications in India:
1. CRZ notifications are issued under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
2. Coastal Zone Management Plans (CZMPs) are prepared by the Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
3. CRZ-I category includes ecologically sensitive areas such as mangroves and coral reefs.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: CRZ notifications are indeed issued under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986. Statement 2 is incorrect: CZMPs are prepared by coastal states/UTs, not directly by the Union Ministry. Statement 3 is correct: CRZ-I includes ecologically sensitive areas like mangroves and coral reefs.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a permanent member (P5) of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The five permanent members (P5) of the UN Security Council are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States. Germany is part of the G4 nations, which advocate for permanent seats but is not currently a permanent member.

prelimsMocks.question: With respect to India's space sector, the term 'IN-SPACe' is associated with:
options: (a) A new propulsion technology developed by ISRO for deep space missions.
(b) An autonomous body established to promote and regulate private sector participation in space activities.
(c) India's first human spaceflight mission module.
(d) A global consortium for lunar exploration led by India.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) An autonomous body established to promote and regulate private sector participation in space activities.
prelimsMocks.explanation: IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre) is an autonomous body under the Department of Space, established to enable and regulate the participation of private sector entities in India's space activities.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) is pivotal for achieving energy security and climate goals. Discuss the strategic importance of NGHM and analyze the challenges India faces in becoming a global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Energy, Infrastructure, Environmental Pollution & Degradation, Conservation, Science & Technology-developments & their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NGHM and its dual goals. Discuss strategic importance (decarbonization, energy security, economic growth, job creation, geopolitical leverage). Analyze challenges (cost, infrastructure, technology, R&D, raw materials, policy consistency, international competition). Conclude with a way forward for India.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent verdict on electoral bond transparency is a significant step towards cleaning political funding. Critically examine the impact of this judgment on India's democratic processes and suggest further reforms needed for transparent electoral financing.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution, Electoral Reforms, Transparency & Accountability.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the context of electoral bonds and the SC verdict. Discuss the positive impacts (increased transparency, citizen's right to know, reduced crony capitalism). Critically analyze potential limitations or new challenges. Suggest comprehensive electoral finance reforms (e.g., state funding, cap on individual donations, stronger ECI powers). Conclude on the necessity of continuous reforms for robust democracy.

mainsMocks.question: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet 21st-century demands. Evaluate the progress made in integrating skill development and vocational education under NEP 2020 and discuss the key challenges in its effective implementation across the country.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources; Government policies and interventions.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NEP 2020 and its vision for skill development. Evaluate progress (vocational course integration, multidisciplinary approach, digital literacy). Discuss key challenges (infrastructure, teacher training, digital divide, curriculum reform, industry linkage, funding). Conclude with a balanced view on the long-term potential and necessary interventions for successful implementation.

quickBytes.text: Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 10 MMT production capacity by 2035.
Supreme Court mandates disclosure of electoral bond donor identities to the ECI.
India's 'Act East Policy' is aimed at extensive engagement with Southeast Asian nations.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark repo rate in India.
CRZ notifications are issued under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
ISRO's RLV-TD program focuses on developing Reusable Launch Vehicle technology.
Project Tiger was launched in India in 1973 for tiger conservation.
The Delimitation Commission redraws constituency boundaries based on census data.
NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The Western Ghats is one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Amendments Ensuring Greater Transparency in Electoral Bonds
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld recent legislative amendments aimed at enhancing transparency in the electoral bond scheme.
• The ruling mandates disclosure of donor identities to the Election Commission of India (ECI) for all future donations.
• This decision comes after prolonged public debate and multiple petitions challenging the opacity of the original scheme.
• Advocates for electoral reforms hail the verdict as a significant step towards cleaning political funding.
• The court emphasized the citizen's right to know and the importance of a level playing field in democratic processes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bond Scheme was introduced in 2017.
Initially, donor identity was kept anonymous.
Supreme Court had previously struck down certain aspects of the original scheme.
The current ruling upholds amendments requiring disclosure of donor details to ECI.
Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression, which includes the right to know.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on electoral funding transparency in India.
Analyze the balance between donor privacy and the public's right to information in political donations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of electoral bonds as a mechanism for political funding and its impact on democratic principles.
Suggest further reforms needed to ensure fair and transparent elections in India.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional values related to democratic governance.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with the Supreme Court of India building in the background, symbolizing judicial pronouncement.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for administering elections in India according to the rules and regulations mentioned in the Constitution of India.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Renewed Focus on Maritime Security and Digital Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit wrapped up, solidifying strategic partnerships in key areas.
• Leaders emphasized enhanced cooperation in maritime domain awareness, counter-piracy, and freedom of navigation.
• Significant agreements signed to boost digital economy integration, cybersecurity, and cross-border digital payments.
• India reiterated its commitment to the Act East Policy, seeking deeper economic and cultural ties with Southeast Asian nations.
• Discussions also covered regional security challenges, climate change, and post-pandemic economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was founded in 1967.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992 and a Full Dialogue Partner in 1995.
India's 'Look East Policy' was upgraded to 'Act East Policy' in 2014.
ASEAN members include Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam.
Maritime security and digital cooperation are increasingly important pillars of India-ASEAN relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of ASEAN for India's Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN nations.
Examine how digital cooperation can drive economic growth and regional integration in Southeast Asia.
Evaluate India's role in addressing common regional challenges such as climate change and geopolitical shifts.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the India-ASEAN comprehensive strategic partnership.
GS2.imageDescription: A composite image showing flags of India and ASEAN member states, with naval ships and digital network lines symbolizing cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The ASEAN Secretariat is located in Jakarta, Indonesia.

GS2.headline: Government Rolls Out Universal Healthcare Access Program Pilot in 10 States
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry launched a pilot phase of its ambitious 'Sarvajan Swasthya Yojana' (Universal Healthcare Scheme) in ten states.
• The program aims to provide comprehensive health coverage, including preventive, promotive, curative, and palliative care, to all citizens.
• It integrates existing national health schemes and focuses on strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure.
• A digital health ID system is being implemented to ensure seamless access to medical records and services.
• The pilot phase will assess implementation challenges and gather feedback before a nationwide rollout.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is a flagship health scheme providing health insurance cover.
National Health Mission (NHM) encompasses both rural and urban health missions.
Digital Health ID is part of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
The pilot program focuses on comprehensive healthcare, including preventive and promotive aspects.
Right to health is often linked to Article 21 (Right to Life) of the Indian Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the need and challenges of achieving universal healthcare access in a diverse country like India.
Analyze how the 'Sarvajan Swasthya Yojana' integrates with and improves upon existing health schemes.
Evaluate the role of digital health infrastructure and data privacy concerns in its implementation.
Examine the potential impact of such a scheme on public health indicators, poverty reduction, and human development.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and quality healthcare delivery across all regions and socio-economic strata.
GS2.imageDescription: A diverse group of people from different age groups and backgrounds receiving medical consultation, symbolizing universal healthcare.
GS2.trivia: India's National Health Policy 2017 aims to achieve the highest possible level of health and well-being for all through a comprehensive healthcare system.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Urgent UN Security Council Reforms at G4 Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister strongly reiterated the call for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council (UNSC) at the G4 meeting.
• The G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, India, and Japan) emphasized the need for an expanded UNSC to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
• Highlighted the underrepresentation of developing countries, particularly from Africa, Latin America, and Asia, in the permanent membership.
• India stressed its credentials for a permanent seat, citing its demographic size, economic power, and commitment to multilateralism.
• The meeting concluded with a joint statement urging accelerated intergovernmental negotiations on UNSC expansion.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: The UN Security Council has 15 members: 5 permanent (P5) and 10 non-permanent.
The P5 are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
Non-permanent members are elected for two-year terms by the UN General Assembly.
G4 nations (Brazil, Germany, India, Japan) advocate for permanent seats for themselves.
India has served multiple terms as a non-permanent member of the UNSC.
mainsPoints: Discuss the arguments for and against the expansion of the UN Security Council's permanent membership.
Analyze India's aspirations for a permanent UNSC seat and the challenges it faces in achieving this goal.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the G4 group in pushing for UNSC reforms and its role in global governance.
Examine how the current structure of the UNSC reflects or fails to reflect contemporary geopolitical realities.
Suggest pathways and strategies for India to strengthen its claim for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations headquarters building in New York, with flags of member states, symbolizing international diplomacy and reform efforts.
GS2.trivia: The UN Charter was signed on 26 June 1945, in San Francisco, at the conclusion of the United Nations Conference on International Organization.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Completes Draft Report for a North-Eastern State, Sparks Debate
GS2.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission submitted its draft report for redrawing assembly and parliamentary constituencies in a key North-Eastern state.
• The exercise aims to rationalize constituency boundaries based on the latest census data and geographical contiguity.
• The report has generated significant political and social debate, with various groups raising concerns over representation and demographic shifts.
• Public hearings are scheduled to gather feedback from stakeholders before the final report is published.
• The delimitation process is crucial for ensuring fair representation in legislative bodies and upholding democratic principles.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Delimitation means the act or process of fixing limits or boundaries of territorial constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission of India.
Its main function is to redraw the boundaries of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituencies.
The last nationwide delimitation exercise was completed in 2002-2008.
Delimitation is done on the basis of the latest census data, but the number of seats for Lok Sabha and Assemblies was frozen until 2026 by the 84th Amendment Act, 2001.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and objectives behind the delimitation exercise in India.
Analyze the socio-political implications of delimitation, particularly in ethnically diverse regions like the North-East.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in ensuring fair and equitable representation.
Examine the potential for conflicts and discontent arising from the redrawing of constituency boundaries.
Suggest measures to make the delimitation process more transparent and acceptable to all stakeholders.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of a North-Eastern state with constituency boundaries being redrawn, symbolizing the delimitation process.
GS2.trivia: The orders of the Delimitation Commission have the force of law and cannot be called in question in any court.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Skill Development Progress
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation found significant progress in integrating skill development.
• The Ministry of Education highlighted increased enrollment in vocational courses and multidisciplinary programs at various levels.
• Focus on experiential learning, critical thinking, and digital literacy has shown positive early outcomes.
• Challenges remain in infrastructure development, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access across all regions.
• The review emphasized continued efforts to align education with industry demands and prepare students for future jobs.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key features include 5+3+3+4 curricular structure, multidisciplinary approach, vocational integration, and flexible learning.
Focuses on foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
Promotes the use of technology in education, including National Educational Technology Forum (NETF).
mainsPoints: Discuss the key objectives and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 for India's education system.
Analyze the progress made in integrating skill development and vocational education as envisioned by NEP.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of NEP 2020, especially concerning teacher training, infrastructure, and digital divide.
Examine how NEP aims to align education with the demands of the 21st-century job market and foster innovation.
Suggest policy measures to ensure equitable access and quality education for all, particularly in rural and remote areas.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on learning activities in a modern classroom, symbolizing skill-based education.
GS2.trivia: India's first National Policy on Education was formulated in 1968, based on the recommendations of the Kothari Commission.

GS2.headline: India and EU Sign Critical Minerals Partnership Agreement, Boosting Supply Chain Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• India and the European Union (EU) formalized a strategic partnership agreement on critical minerals, aiming to diversify supply chains.
• The pact focuses on joint exploration, sustainable extraction, processing, and recycling of crucial raw materials.
• Recognizes the importance of critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earths for green and digital transitions.
• Aims to reduce dependency on single-source suppliers and enhance economic security for both regions.
• Establishes a framework for technology transfer, research collaboration, and investment in the critical minerals sector.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Critical minerals are essential for modern technologies and economic growth, particularly in green and digital sectors.
Examples include lithium (EV batteries), cobalt (EV batteries), and rare earth elements (electronics).
India and the EU are both heavily reliant on imports for many critical minerals.
This partnership aims to reduce geopolitical risks associated with concentrated supply chains.
The EU has its own Critical Raw Materials Act to secure supply.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth and national security in the context of global supply chain vulnerabilities.
Analyze how the India-EU partnership on critical minerals can enhance supply chain resilience and foster a sustainable green transition for both regions.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in joint exploration, extraction, processing, and recycling of critical minerals.
Examine the geopolitical implications of such partnerships in reducing dependency on dominant suppliers.
Suggest policy frameworks for India to build domestic capabilities in critical mineral value chains and promote circular economy principles.
